description: IP 91.200.81.47 appears in email_1 X-Originating-IP header AND network_logs outbound at 02:11 AM — same bulk mailer infrastructure. This is a valid cross-evidence link.
forensicLesson: IP address correlation across evidence sources strengthens attribution — but requires timestamps to confirm simultaneity.

description: audio_call.mp3 creation timestamp 02:14 AM matches the Tor exit node connection in network_logs at 02:14 AM — strong temporal co-occurrence.
forensicLesson: Timestamp alignment across unrelated evidence types (audio metadata + firewall logs) creates a strong temporal chain — but correlation alone does not prove causation.

description: invoice_fraud.pdf was modified at 14:15. The 4.7 MB exfiltration in network_logs occurred 8 hours earlier at 02:14 AM — temporal proximity exists but is insufficient to prove same actor.
forensicLesson: Temporal proximity is weak evidence. Without a direct data artifact linking the invoice file to the exfiltrated payload, this connection is circumstantial.
